data.title: Dry Ground AI: New Features for Ministry Teams
data.excerpt: Discover Dry Ground AI's latest features for generating sermons, devotionals, and church communications. Perfect for Christian content teams.

$media.alt: Dry Ground AI: New Features for Ministry Teams

children.text: If you're part of a church staff, a Christian nonprofit, or a ministry team that spends countless hours crafting messages, you know the constant pressure. You need to communicate with clarity, theological soundness, and relevance—week after week. What if you could have a creative partner that understands your mission? Enter Dry Ground AI, and they've just rolled out some genuinely exciting new features that deserve a closer look.

As someone who's always scanning for tools that respect domain expertise, I was impressed. This isn't a generic AI; it's built from the ground up for faith-based communication. Let's break down what's new and why it might be the operational upgrade your team needs.
What's New and Exciting in Dry Ground AI?
The platform has focused its recent updates on depth, context, and workflow integration—exactly what busy ministry teams need.
1. Sermon Series Builder:

This is a standout. You're not just generating a single sermon draft. The new builder lets you input a theme, a book of the Bible, or a core doctrine, and it helps structure a multi-week sermon series. It suggests a logical progression, key scriptures for each part, and even potential small group discussion questions that tie back to the weekly message. It’s like having a seasoned sermon prep assistant who ensures continuity and depth across weeks.
2. Context-Aware Devotional Generator:

Devotionals require a different tone—more intimate, more reflective. The updated generator now allows you to specify the audience (e.g., new believers, women's ministry, youth group) and the desired length (a 5-day plan or a 30-day journal). It produces content that feels personally written, not algorithmically generic. I tested it for a 7-day plan on 'Hope in Hard Times,' and the theological consistency and pastoral warmth were remarkable.
See how this fits into your workflow
3. Integrated Brand Voice & Doctrine Settings:

This is huge for organizational cohesion. You can now upload your church's or organization's statement of faith, key doctrinal positions, and even preferred phrases or terms to avoid. Dry Ground AI then learns this "brand voice," ensuring that every piece of content—be it a social media post, a newsletter, or a flyer—aligns perfectly with your core beliefs and communication style. No more off-message drafts.
Understand why this is a game-changer
4. Enhanced Communication Toolkit:

Need a volunteer recruitment email? A fundraising appeal? A community event announcement? The toolkit now includes more specialized templates that are tuned for the unique challenges and opportunities of church communication. It asks for the goal (awareness, action, giving) and the audience, then generates compelling copy that feels authentic and mission-driven.
Who Is Dry Ground AI Perfect For?
This tool isn't for everyone, and it's proud of that. Its ideal users are specific and passionate.
Pastors and Teaching Teams:

Drowning in sermon prep but wanting to reclaim time for pastoral care and counseling? The Sermon Series Builder and outline tools are a lifeline. You maintain theological oversight; the AI handles the heavy lifting of structuring and initial drafting.
Communications Directors & Staff:

Juggling social media, email newsletters, bulletins, and website copy? The doctrine-aware generator ensures every piece is on-brand and theologically sound, freeing you to focus on strategy and community engagement.
Explore its use cases in detail
Christian Nonprofit Leaders:

From donor appeals to impact reports, you need to tell your story with power and clarity. Dry Ground AI helps you craft messages that resonate with supporters and clearly articulate your mission's value.
Ministry Volunteers:

Writing a devotional for a small group or a blog post for the church website? This tool gives you a confident starting point, ensuring your contribution is solid and aligned with the wider church vision.
Ready to see it in action?
Try Dry Ground AI through this link
to experience the difference firsthand.
A Step-by-Step Look at the New Workflow
Getting started is straightforward. Here’s how a typical team might leverage the new features:
Define Your Voice:
First, spend 30 minutes uploading your key doctrinal documents and style guide into the new "Brand Voice" section. This is the foundation for everything.
Plan Your Content Calendar:
Use the Sermon Series Builder to map out the next quarter's preaching themes. Export the series outline and discussion questions to share with your teaching team and small group leaders.
Generate Core Drafts:
For each week, use the sermon generator to create a detailed draft. Use the devotional tool to create a companion 5-day reading plan for the congregation to engage with the theme all week.
Craft Communications:
Use the Communication Toolkit to draft the weekly newsletter that highlights the sermon theme, promotes the devotional, and includes a call to action for an upcoming ministry event.
Collaborate and Refine:

The drafts land in your shared workspace. Your team adds personal stories, illustrations, and final edits. The AI did 80% of the structural and research work, freeing your team to add the irreplaceable 20% of heart and experience.
Get pro tips for best results
The platform operates on a subscription model through
PartnerStack
, with tiers starting at $497/month for Pro plans. It’s an investment, but for the right team, it replaces hours of manual work and creative block.
My Final Take: It's About Amplifying, Not Replacing

Let's be clear: Dry Ground AI doesn't write the sermon you preach. It doesn't replace your prayer or study. What it does is remove the biggest bottlenecks in content creation: the blank page, the structural scramble, and the brand inconsistency.

The new features show a deep understanding of ministry pain points. The Series Builder solves the "what's next?" problem. The Doctrine Settings solve the "is this on-message?" worry. It’s a tool built with respect for the sacred work you do.
If your team's creativity is being sapped by administrative content production, and you want to invest more time in people and discipleship, this platform is worth a serious evaluation.
Start your journey with Dry Ground AI here

children.text: Is Dry Ground AI theologically safe?

Dry Ground AI is designed as a content assistant, not a theologian. Its key safety feature is the new "Brand Voice & Doctrine Settings," where you upload your own statement of faith and guidelines. This constrains the AI's outputs to align with your specific beliefs. The final responsibility for doctrinal accuracy always rests with your human leadership team who review and edit all content before publication.
Can it generate content in languages other than English?

Currently, Dry Ground AI's primary language support is English. The platform uses advanced large language models that can understand and generate text in other languages, but the fine-tuned sermon, devotional, and church communication templates are most robust in English. Support for additional languages is a common request and may be on their development roadmap.
How is this different from using ChatGPT for church content?

While you can prompt a general AI like ChatGPT, Dry Ground AI is a specialized platform. It offers structured templates (like sermon series builders), persistent brand voice training, and a workflow built for teams. It saves you from writing long, complex prompts every time and ensures consistent, ministry-focused output out of the box, rather than generic content that may miss the nuances of faith-based communication.
